1-naive.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries for Venous Thromboembolism (VTE) Diagnoses

### SNOMED CT — Hierarchical Search (covers DVT + PE under VTE parent concept)
```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|111293003&_include=Condition:patient
```

### SNOMED CT — Explicit Codes (Pulmonary Embolism + DVT)
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|59282003&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|128053003&_include=Condition:patient
```

### ICD-10-CM — Pulmonary Embolism (I26.x)
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I26&_include=Condition:patient
```

### ICD-10-CM — DVT of Lower Extremity (I82.4x)
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I82.4&_include=Condition:patient
```

### ICD-10-CM — DVT of Upper Extremity (I82.6x)
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I82.6&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### Notes

| Query | Scope |
|---|---|
| SNOMED `111293003` (`:below`) | Umbrella VTE concept; includes PE, DVT lower/upper as descendants |
| SNOMED `59282003` | Pulmonary embolism (explicit) |
| SNOMED `128053003` | Deep vein thrombosis (all sites) |
| ICD-10 `I26` (`:below`) | All PE/infarction subcodes (I26.01, I26.09, I26.90, I26.99, etc.) |
| ICD-10 `I82.4` (`:below`) | DVT lower extremity subcodes (I82.401–I82.4Z9, etc.) |
| ICD-10 `I82.6` (`:below`) | DVT upper extremity subcodes (I82.601–I82.62Z, etc.) |

- The **`:below` modifier** traverses code system hierarchy to capture all subcodes — server must support it (FHIR R4+).
- `_include=Condition:patient` returns linked **Patient** resources in the same Bundle.
- For chronic/resolved cases, append `&clinical-status=active` (active) or omit to include all statuses.

```

**Coverage Notes:**

| Query | Codes Covered |
|---|---|
| SNOMED `234049000` | Venous thromboembolism (parent concept) |
| SNOMED `59282003` | Pulmonary embolism |
| SNOMED `128053003` | Deep vein thrombosis (all sites) |
| ICD-10 `I26` + `:below` | I26.01, I26.02, I26.09, I26.90, I26.92–I26.99 |
| ICD-10 `I82.4` + `:below` | Acute DVT — femoral, iliac, popliteal, tibial, peroneal, calf |
| ICD-10 `I82.5` + `:below` | Chronic DVT — lower extremity equivalents |
| ICD-10 `I82.62/I82.72` + `:below` | Acute/chronic DVT — brachial, radial, ulnar (bilateral & unilateral) |

> **Note:** The `:below` modifier relies on hierarchical code system support in the terminology server. `_include=Condition:patient` retrieves the linked Patient resources in the same bundle.

2-naive.raw_response:
The count is stable at 1,449 — all records are consistently multi-coded (SNOMED + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM). I now have the complete picture to compile the final comprehensive query.

**Summary of approach:**
- **SNOMED CT** (79 codes from VSAC Joint Commission "Venous Thromboembolism" value set, OID `2.16.840.1.113883.3.117.1.7.1.330`) + `128054004` (upper extremity DVT)
- **ICD-10-CM**: All 56 VSAC codes + `I26.90/I26.91/I26.01` (common PE codes omitted from quality-measure set) + `I82.40`, `I82.441–I82.499` (additional laterality variants seen in server samples) + full **upper extremity DVT** block (`I82.6xx` / `I82.7xx` / `I82.Axx` axillary / `I82.Bxx` subclavian / `I82.Cxx` internal jugular) + chronic lower extremity DVT (`I82.5xx`)

```

|-------------|-----------|

| `234049002` | Axillary vein thrombosis |

---

### Client-Side Set Difference Logic

```
Set A = { MedicationRequest[*].subject.reference }  ← from Query 1
Set B = { Condition[*].subject.reference }           ← from Query 2

Result = Set A \ Set B  (patients in A NOT present in B)
```

**Pseudocode:**
```python
set_A = {r["subject"]["reference"] for r in med_requests}
set_B = {r["subject"]["reference"] for r in conditions}
anticoag_without_vte = set_A - set_B
```

---

### Design Notes

| Consideration | Detail |
|---|---|
| `_elements=subject` | Minimizes payload — only returns patient references, not full resources |
| `status=active` | Filters to *current* anticoag prescriptions only (remove if historical needed) |
| Pagination | Both queries may return `Bundle.link[next]` — **must exhaust all pages** before computing the set difference |
| System prefix per token | Each comma-separated token value carries its own `system\|code` — do not rely on shared-prefix shorthand (not portable across FHIR servers) |
| `clinicalStatus` on Condition | Consider adding `&clinical-status=active` to Query 2 if resolved/historical VTE should be excluded from Set B |

2-naive.prompt_text:
I need to identify my complete venous thromboembolism patient population for a quality improvement initiative on anticoagulation duration and bleeding events. From clinical experience, VTE patients are documented in different ways: most have a formal VTE diagnosis (DVT, PE, or extent-specific codes like iliofemoral DVT), most are on therapeutic anticoagulants (warfarin, DOACs like apixaban or rivaroxaban, or enoxaparin), and many had elevated D-dimer at presentation.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any VTE diagnosis (DVT, iliofemoral DVT, IVC thrombosis, PE, or venous embolism)
2. Patients prescribed therapeutic anticoagulants (warfarin, apixaban, rivaroxaban, dabigatran, or enoxaparin)
3. Patients with elevated D-dimer (>0.5 mcg/mL FEU)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found.

2-broad.raw_response:
All three queries are validated and stable. Here's my complete summary of what was discovered and the final queries:

---

## 🔍 Discovery Summary

| Domain | Code Systems Found | Source(s) Used | Count |
|--------|-------------------|----------------|-------|
| **VTE Diagnoses** | SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM (all co-present per resource) | VSAC Joint Commission VTE set (2026 edition, 79 SNOMED + 56 ICD-10-CM) | **1,601** |
| **Anticoagulants** | RxNorm ingredient-level codes (e.g., `rivaroxaban`, `dabigatran etexilate`) | VSAC BWH Anticoagulant Medications (2025 edition, 150 codes) | **2,169** |
| **Elevated D-dimer** | LOINC `48065-7` (FEU, platelet-poor plasma) — units stored as µg/mL | Server sample + unit probe | **272** |

### Key Server Findings
- **FHIR R4** (Azure FHIR Server 4.0.728), no US Core IG loaded
- Every Condition carries **SNOMED + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM** simultaneously → combining systems yields comprehensive coverage without inflating counts (all three systems overlap on the same resource)
- MedicationRequests use **RxNorm ingredient-level codes** (e.g., `1114195` rivaroxaban, NOT clinical drug form codes), though form codes are included for completeness
- D-dimer values stored in **µg/mL FEU**; the clinical cutoff of `>0.5 µg/mL` returns 272 confirmed elevated results from 2,401 total D-dimer tests
- `value-quantity=gt0.5` unitless and `gt0.5||ug/mL` both return 272 — the server correctly interprets µg/mL UCUM units

### Code Coverage Detail

**VTE Diagnoses (SNOMED — 22 key codes):** DVT (128053003), PE (59282003), Iliofemoral DVT (234044007), IVC thrombosis (281595001), Venous embolism (234049002), DVT lower extremity (404223003), DVT pelvic vein (128055005), Acute iliofemoral DVT (132301000119107), Vena cava embolism (50817002 / 195437003 / 83938003), Thromboembolism of vein (429098002), PE subtypes (233935004, 233936003, 1001000119102), Bilateral DVT (444325005, 285441000119102, 285451000119100, 285461000119103), Iliac vein thrombosis (762256003), Deep thrombophlebitis (16750002, 40198004)

**VTE Diagnoses (ICD-10-CM — 72 codes):** I82.220 (IVC), I82.401–I82.4Y9 (DVT subtypes by laterality/segment), I26.02–I26.99 (PE subtypes), I80.10–I80.299 (femoral, iliac, popliteal, deep vessel phlebitis/thrombophlebitis), I82.890, I82.90 (unspecified)

**Anticoagulants (RxNorm):** Warfarin (11289 / 114194 / 405155 Jantoven), Apixaban (1364430 / 1364436 Eliquis / 1364445 / 1364447), Rivaroxaban (1114195 / 1114199 Xarelto / 2059015 / 2059017), Dabigatran (1037041 / 1037042 / 1037045 / 1037046 Pradaxa / 1037179 / 1723476), Enoxaparin (67108 / 221095 / 225036 Lovenox / 854228–854252 prefilled syringes)

**D-dimer:** LOINC 48065-7 (platelet-poor plasma FEU) + 48067-3 (citrated plasma FEU), threshold >0.5 µg/mL

---

## ✅ Final FHIR Queries (Union = Full VTE Population)

```
// QUERY 1 — VTE Diagnoses: 1,601 resources
// VSAC Joint Commission "Venous Thromboembolism" value set (2026 edition)
// SNOMED CT (22 concept codes) + ICD-10-CM (72 codes spanning DVT, PE, IVC thrombosis, venous embolism, iliac/femoral/popliteal thrombophlebitis)
```

```
// QUERY 2 — Therapeutic Anticoagulants: 2,169 resources
// VSAC BWH "Anticoagulant Medications" (2025 edition) filtered to the 5 VTE drugs
// Warfarin + Apixaban + Rivaroxaban + Dabigatran + Enoxaparin (ingredient + brand + formulation RxNorm codes)
```

```
// QUERY 3 — Elevated D-dimer: 272 resources
// LOINC 48065-7 (FEU, platelet-poor plasma) + 48067-3 (FEU, citrated plasma)
// Threshold: >0.5 µg/mL FEU per standard clinical cutoff
```

I need to identify my complete venous thromboembolism patient population for a quality improvement initiative on anticoagulation duration and bleeding events. From clinical experience, VTE patients are documented in different ways: most have a formal VTE diagnosis (DVT, PE, or extent-specific codes like iliofemoral DVT), most are on therapeutic anticoagulants (warfarin, DOACs like apixaban or rivaroxaban, or enoxaparin), and many had elevated D-dimer at presentation.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any VTE diagnosis (DVT, iliofemoral DVT, IVC thrombosis, PE, or venous embolism)
2. Patients prescribed therapeutic anticoagulants (warfarin, apixaban, rivaroxaban, dabigatran, or enoxaparin)
3. Patients with elevated D-dimer (>0.5 mcg/mL FEU)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found.

2-expert.raw_response:
Counts stable at 1,601 / 981 / 272 — the additional codes hit the same resources, confirming the code sets are fully saturated. All queries validated. Let me now write the complete final queries.

---

## 🔬 Full VTE Population Query — 3-Arm Coverage

Here's a complete walkthrough of what was discovered before presenting the final URLs:

---

### What the investigation found

| Layer | Finding |
|---|---|
| **FHIR Server** | Azure FHIR R4 (4.0.1) — no US Core IG loaded |
| **Condition coding** | Triple-coded: SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM (all confirmed in samples) |
| **MedicationRequest coding** | RxNorm **ingredient-level** codes (e.g., `1114195` = rivaroxaban, `1037042` = dabigatran etexilate, `67108` = enoxaparin) **plus** dose-specific codes |
| **Observation coding** | LOINC `48065-7` — Fibrin D-dimer FEU [Mass/volume], confirmed on every sampled record |
| **VTE VSAC value set** | Joint Commission (2025/2026): **79 SNOMED** + **56 ICD-10-CM** codes — fully authoritative |
| **Anticoagulant VSAC** | Joint Commission "Anticoagulant Therapy" (2025): **57 dose-specific RxNorm** codes covering all 5 drugs |
| **D-dimer threshold** | `>0.5 µg/mL FEU` → FHIR `value-quantity=gt0.5||ug/mL` ✓ |

---

### Validated counts

| Arm | Resources matched |
|---|---|
| VTE Diagnoses (Condition) | **1,601** |
| Anticoagulant Prescriptions (MedicationRequest) | **981** |
| Elevated D-dimer (Observation) | **272** |

---

### Final FHIR Queries

2-expert.prompt_text:
I need to identify my complete venous thromboembolism patient population for a quality improvement initiative on anticoagulation duration and bleeding events. From clinical experience, VTE patients are documented in different ways: most have a formal VTE diagnosis (DVT, PE, or extent-specific codes like iliofemoral DVT), most are on therapeutic anticoagulants (warfarin, DOACs like apixaban or rivaroxaban, or enoxaparin), and many had elevated D-dimer at presentation.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any VTE diagnosis (DVT, iliofemoral DVT, IVC thrombosis, PE, or venous embolism)
2. Patients prescribed therapeutic anticoagulants (warfarin, apixaban, rivaroxaban, dabigatran, or enoxaparin)
3. Patients with elevated D-dimer (>0.5 mcg/mL FEU)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found.

3-naive.raw_response:
All four queries validated. Unit-qualified D-dimer matches (272 both with/without explicit unit, confirming `ug/mL` is the stored unit). Now I have everything needed for the final answer.

**Server confirmed:** Conditions carry SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM codings. MedicationRequests use RxNorm. Observations use LOINC. D-dimer is stored in `ug/mL` FEU.

From clinical experience, VTE patients are documented in different ways: most have a formal VTE diagnosis (DVT, PE, or extent-specific codes like iliofemoral DVT), most are on therapeutic anticoagulants (warfarin, DOACs like apixaban or rivaroxaban, or enoxaparin), and many had elevated D-dimer at presentation.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any VTE diagnosis (DVT, iliofemoral DVT, IVC thrombosis, PE, or venous embolism)
2. Patients prescribed therapeutic anticoagulants (warfarin, apixaban, rivaroxaban, dabigatran, or enoxaparin)
3. Patients with elevated D-dimer (>0.5 mcg/mL FEU)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found.

All three queries are verified and validated. Here is my complete summary and final answer.

---

## 🔬 VTE Population Query — Full Methodology Report

### Playbooks Applied
- **Playbook 1 (Subtypes):** DVT, iliofemoral DVT, IVC thrombosis, PE, venous embolism all queried as a union
- **Playbook 8 (Multi-system code lists):** Single Condition query combining SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM
- **Playbook 7 (Threshold):** D-dimer `value-quantity=gt0.5|ug/mL FEU`
- **Playbook 12 (Cohort = OR union):** Three separate resource-type queries whose patient sets are unioned

---

|---|---|
| `Condition` | SNOMED CT **+** ICD-10-CM **+** ICD-9-CM (all present per resource) |
| `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm |
| `Observation` | LOINC 48065-7 (D-dimer FEU, unit: `ug/mL`) |

| **ICD-10-CM** | I26.09, I26.92–I26.94, I26.99 (PE variants); I82.220 (IVC); I82.401–I82.439 (DVT femoral/iliac/popliteal/unspecified); I82.890, I82.90 |
| **ICD-9-CM** | 415.11, 415.13, 415.19 (PE); 451.11–451.9 (thrombophlebitis); 453.2 (IVC), 453.40–453.9 (venous embolism/thrombosis) |

### 💡 Usage Notes for Your QI Initiative
- **Union logic:** Take the **patient-level union** of all three query result sets. A patient qualifies if they appear in *any* of the three.
- **Anticoagulant query is broadest** (2,169 resources) — expected, as it also captures patients being treated for AF or other indications. For a purer VTE-only anticoagulation cohort, use Playbook 11 (`_has` intersection) to AND the diagnosis + medication queries.
- **D-dimer query is narrowest** (272) — this primarily captures acute/recent presentations. Use it to enrich the cohort, not exclude patients who may have presented without lab work.
- **ICD-9-CM codes are critical** (1,285 standalone records) — excluding them would lose a significant portion of your historical VTE population.

1-expert.raw_response:
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|128053003,http://snomed.info/sct|234044007,http://snomed.info/sct|281595001,http://snomed.info/sct|59282003,http://snomed.info/sct|234049002
```

**Breakdown of the query:**

| SNOMED Code | Concept | VTE Category |
|---|---|---|
| `128053003` | Deep venous thrombosis (parent) | DVT |
| `234044007` | Iliofemoral DVT | DVT – extent-specific |
| `281595001` | Inferior vena cava thrombosis | DVT – extent-specific |
| `59282003` | Pulmonary embolism (parent) | PE |
| `234049002` | Venous embolism NOS | Catch-all |

**Design notes:**
- All five codes are combined into a **single query** using comma separation on the `code` parameter, which FHIR interprets as a logical **OR** — no need for five separate round trips.
- The system URI `http://snomed.info/sct` is consistently applied to each code token, ensuring the search is system-bound and won't collide with identically-numbered codes in other systems.
- If your server supports `$expand` + value set binding, you could alternatively reference the Joint Commission OID directly via `Condition?code:in=urn:oid:2.16.840.1.113883.3.117.1.7.1.330`, but the explicit code list above is broadly portable across FHIR R4 servers regardless of terminology service capability.
- Consider adding `&clinical-status=active` or `&verification-status=confirmed` to further refine results to confirmed, active VTE diagnoses if clinically appropriate.

1-naive.raw_response:
Here are FHIR queries to find patients with high D-dimer observations, using common LOINC codes and filtering for elevated/high interpretation or abnormal values:

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|48066-5&interpretation=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ObservationInterpretation|H&_include=Observation:patient
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|48067-3&interpretation=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ObservationInterpretation|H&_include=Observation:patient
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|71427-9&interpretation=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ObservationInterpretation|H&_include=Observation:patient
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3255-7&interpretation=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ObservationInterpretation|H&_include=Observation:patient
```

### Notes:
| LOINC Code | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| `48066-5` | Fibrin D-dimer DDU \[Mass/vol\] in Platelet poor plasma |
| `48067-3` | Fibrin D-dimer FEU \[Mass/vol\] in Platelet poor plasma |
| `71427-9` | Fibrin D-dimer DDU \[Mass/vol\] in Blood by Immunoassay |
| `3255-7` | Fibrin D-dimer \[Units/vol\] in Platelet poor plasma |

- **Interpretation `H`** flags values above the lab's reference range (typically **> 0.5 mg/L DDU** or **> 500 ng/mL**).
- You can also filter by `HH` (critically high) by replacing `|H` with `|HH`.
- `_include=Observation:patient` pulls the associated **Patient** resources in the same bundle.
- If you prefer value-based filtering (e.g., DDU > 0.5 mg/L), you can add: `&value-quantity=gt0.5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|mg/L`

1-broad.raw_response:
## D-Dimer Elevated Observations (> 0.5 mcg/mL FEU)

**Key notes:**
- **LOINC codes used** cover FEU (Fibrinogen Equivalent Units) specifically, as requested
- **Unit:** `ug/mL` is the UCUM code for mcg/mL (1 mcg/mL = 1 mg/L = 1 ug/mL)
- **Threshold:** `gt0.5` corresponds to the standard VTE workup cutoff (0.5 mcg/mL FEU)
- Multiple LOINC codes are queried to cover common D-dimer FEU assay variants

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|48065-7&value-quantity=gt0.5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|ug/mL&status=final,amended&_include=Observation:patient

Observation?code=http://loinc.org|71427-9&value-quantity=gt0.5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|ug/mL&status=final,amended&_include=Observation:patient

Observation?code=http://loinc.org|91556-1&value-quantity=gt0.5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|ug/mL&status=final,amended&_include=Observation:patient
```

---

### LOINC Code Reference

| LOINC | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `48065-7` | Fibrin D-dimer **FEU** [Mass/vol] in Platelet poor plasma *(most common)* |
| `71427-9` | Fibrin D-dimer **FEU** [Mass/vol] in Blood by Immunoassay |
| `91556-1` | Fibrin D-dimer **FEU** [Mass/vol] in Platelet poor plasma by Immunoassay |

> ⚠️ **Note on units:** Some labs report D-dimer in **DDU** (D-dimer Units) — the DDU cutoff is **~0.25 mg/L** (equivalent to 0.5 FEU). If your system uses DDU units, query `LOINC|48066-5` with `gt0.25` instead.

## Two-Stage FHIR Query Strategy

> **⚠️ FHIR has no native NOT/exclusion operator across resource types.** This requires two stages:
> 1. **Collect** patient IDs from anticoagulant prescriptions (Step 1)
> 2. **Collect** patient IDs with active VTE/DVT/PE diagnoses (Step 2)
> 3. **Subtract** Step 2 IDs from Step 1 IDs in application logic

---

### 🔵 STEP 1 — Active Anticoagulant Prescriptions (collect Patient IDs)

```
# 1a — Warfarin (VKA)
MedicationRequest?status=active&intent=order&code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|11289&_include=MedicationRequest:patient

# 1b — DOACs: apixaban (Eliquis) | rivaroxaban (Xarelto) | dabigatran (Pradaxa) | edoxaban (Savaysa)
MedicationRequest?status=active&intent=order&code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1364430,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1114195,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1037042,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1599538&_include=MedicationRequest:patient

# 1c — LMWH: enoxaparin (Lovenox) | dalteparin (Fragmin)
MedicationRequest?status=active&intent=order&code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|67108,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|35458&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

---

### 🔴 STEP 2 — Active VTE / DVT / PE on Problem List (EXCLUDE these Patient IDs)

```
# 2a — SNOMED CT: subsumption under VTE parent (111293003) catches DVT + PE + all subtypes
Condition?category=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-category|problem-list-item&clinical-status=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-clinical|active&code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|111293003&_include=Condition:patient

# 2b — SNOMED CT: explicit core codes (DVT | PE | VTE) — fallback if :below not supported
Condition?category=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-category|problem-list-item&clinical-status=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-clinical|active&code=http://snomed.info/sct|128053003,http://snomed.info/sct|59282003,http://snomed.info/sct|111293003&_include=Condition:patient

# 2c — ICD-10-CM: Pulmonary Embolism (I26.x) via hierarchy
Condition?category=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-category|problem-list-item&clinical-status=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-clinical|active&code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I26&_include=Condition:patient

# 2d — ICD-10-CM: DVT acute (I82.4x) + chronic (I82.5x) + upper extremity (I82.6x) via hierarchy
Condition?category=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-category|problem-list-item&clinical-status=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-clinical|active&code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I82.4,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I82.5,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I82.6&_include=Condition:patient

# 2e — ICD-10-CM: Explicit PE codes (use if :below unsupported)
Condition?category=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-category|problem-list-item&clinical-status=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-clinical|active&code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I26.01,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I26.02,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I26.09,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I26.90,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I26.92,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I26.93,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I26.94,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|I26.99&_include=Condition:patient
```

1-expert.raw_response:
## Two-Query Strategy for Client-Side NOT EXISTS

**Step 1 — Fetch anticoagulant MedicationRequests (with patient refs)**
**Step 2 — Fetch VTE Conditions (patient exclusion list)**
**Client-side — Subtract: keep only MedicationRequests whose `subject` does NOT appear in the VTE patient set**

---

```
# ── Query 1: Anticoagulant MedicationRequests ──────────────────────────────
# RxNorm codes: warfarin|11289, apixaban|1364430, rivaroxaban|1114195,
#               dabigatran|1037042, enoxaparin|67108
# _include pulls Patient resources so you have subject.reference for the join

MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|11289,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1364430,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1114195,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1037042,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|67108&_include=MedicationRequest:subject&_elements=id,subject,medicationCodeableConcept,status,authoredOn

# ── Query 2: VTE Conditions (build the exclusion set of Patient IDs) ────────
# SNOMED codes: DVT|128053003, PE|59282003, VTE|234044007,
#               chronic DVT|281595001, chronic VTE|234049002

Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|128053003,http://snomed.info/sct|234044007,http://snomed.info/sct|281595001,http://snomed.info/sct|59282003,http://snomed.info/sct|234049002&_include=Condition:subject&_elements=id,subject,code,clinicalStatus
```

---

### Client-Side Logic (pseudocode)

```python
# After paging through both result sets:

anticoag_med_requests = results_from_query_1   # list of MedicationRequest resources
vte_conditions        = results_from_query_2   # list of Condition resources

# Build exclusion set
vte_patient_ids = {
    c["subject"]["reference"]          # e.g. "Patient/123"
    for c in vte_conditions
}

# Filter: anticoagulants WITHOUT a VTE diagnosis
non_vte_anticoag = [
    mr for mr in anticoag_med_requests
    if mr["subject"]["reference"] not in vte_patient_ids
]
```

---

### Key Design Notes

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **OR within a parameter** | Comma-separated values = OR logic per FHIR spec §3.1.3 — single round-trip per query |
| **`_include`** | Pulls `Patient` bundles alongside results so references are resolvable client-side |
| **`_elements`** | Reduces payload; expand if you need dosing or prescriber fields |
| **Pagination** | Both queries may return large sets — follow `Bundle.link[rel=next]` until exhausted before performing the set subtraction |
| **Clinical status** | Add `&clinical-status=active` to Query 2 if you only want to exclude *active* VTE diagnoses (historical resolved VTE may be intentional anticoagulation indication) |
| **Medication status** | Add `&status=active,completed` to Query 1 to scope to meaningful prescriptions |

FHIR servers don't natively support 'NOT EXISTS' subqueries, so this is best done as two queries (anticoagulant patients minus VTE-diagnosed patients) client-side.

2-broad.prompt_text: Find patients prescribed therapeutic anticoagulants (warfarin, DOACs like apixaban or rivaroxaban, or LMWH like enoxaparin) who do NOT have a formal VTE/DVT/PE diagnosis on their problem list — typically indicating use for AFib, mechanical valves, or post-op prophylaxis.

2-expert.prompt_text: I need MedicationRequest resources for any anticoagulant (warfarin 11289, apixaban 1364430, rivaroxaban 1114195, dabigatran 1037042, enoxaparin 67108) WHERE the patient does NOT have a Condition with VTE codes (128053003, 234044007, 281595001, 59282003, 234049002). FHIR servers don't natively support 'NOT EXISTS' subqueries, so this is best done as two queries (anticoagulant patients minus VTE-diagnosed patients) client-side.

3-naive.prompt_text: Find patients on blood thinners without a clot diagnosis.
